understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete levels by reaching the goal gem tile (+), which is always located in the gravity direction. I needed to use horizontal moves to position myself, let gravity carry me downward to new sections, and use clicks to break blocks (x) that blocked my fall path, while avoiding spike hazards (u/v).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement is only horizontal: MOVE_LEFT or MOVE_RIGHT. After each horizontal move, gravity acts in the current direction (here DOWN, toward lower y-coordinates, i.e., toward the top of the grid) until the player lands on a solid tile, hits a hazard, or reaches the goal. CLICK removes breakable blocks at a specified [x,y] coordinate, which is useful for clearing a vertical fall path when a breakable block is directly above/below. UNDO reverts the last action. Corridor scans show landing positions after a move from each column, and moving toward a passage column (where landing y changes drastically) is the key to progressing to new sections. Spikes (u/v) instantly kill on landing. Each level gives 2 attempts with 50 moves each; only horizontal moves consume the timer, clicks do not. The game ends when either moves are exhausted, the player hits a spike, or the goal is reached.

Corridor scans show landing positions after a move from each column, and moving toward a passage column (where landing y changes drastically) is the key to progressing to new sections. Spikes (u/v) instantly kill on landing. Each level gives 2 attempts with 50 moves each; only horizontal moves consume the timer, clicks do not. The game ends when either moves are exhausted, the player hits a spike, or the goal is reached.
